# monad-gas

> Gas estimator CLI for Monad blockchain. Shows gas limit costs, waste estimates, and compares Monad vs Ethereum.

## Why this exists

Monad charges gas based on gas_limit, not gas_used. Unlike Ethereum, there are no refunds for unused gas. This makes gas estimation accuracy critical: setting gas_limit too high wastes MON, setting it too low reverts the transaction.

## Install

```
npx monad-gas
```

## Key features

- Estimates gas for 6 common transaction presets (transfer, erc20, swap, nft, deploy)
- Shows gas_limit (what you pay) vs estimated gas_used (what you actually use)
- Calculates waste percentage and wasted MON
- Compares Monad costs against Ethereum L1
- Configurable gas limit multiplier (tighter = cheaper but riskier)
- JSON output for CI/CD pipelines
- Programmatic API for integration

## Monad gas model

```
cost = gas_price x gas_limit (no refunds for unused gas)
```

This differs from Ethereum where unused gas is refunded. The --multiplier flag controls buffer above estimated usage (default 1.2 = 20% buffer).

## CLI options

--rpc <url>         Monad RPC endpoint (default: https://rpc.monad.xyz)
--eth-rpc <url>     Ethereum RPC for comparison
--no-compare        Skip Ethereum comparison
--json              JSON output
--mon-price <usd>   MON price for USD conversion
--eth-price <usd>   ETH price for USD conversion
--multiplier <n>    Gas limit multiplier (default: 1.2)
-p, --preset <type> Specific preset: transfer, erc20-transfer, erc20-approve, swap, nft-mint, deploy
--to <address>      Custom transaction target
--data <hex>        Custom transaction calldata

## API

```typescript
import { estimateGas, compareGas } from "monad-gas";

const estimate = await estimateGas(
  { to: "0x...", data: "0x..." },
  { monadRpc: "https://rpc.monad.xyz", gasLimitMultiplier: 1.2 }
);
```

## Related

- monad-audit: Solidity contract scanner for Monad (github.com/LoserLab/monad-audit)
